author: @linuxonbute
text: The word factoid originally meant misinformation repeated so many times it gets thought of as true. A BBC news presenter started using it mistakenly to give little items of fact hence proving it's original definition was correct :<)

text: Agreed

author: @toml.8210
text:
I like tea and coffee...
James Burke said in one of his "Connections" shows, instant coffee was developed during WW2 and was a staple of ever G.I.s diet., along with chocolate bars.

author: @carlturner3600
text:
Hi there Robert. 
I would take my hat off for you, but I don’t wear 1 😂. Can I ask you were are you from you look very familiar from the first video of yours I ever watched I was hooked. Just like to know if I have seen you around. It could just be your white hair exactly same white highlights as me 😂 whiter than snow me. Love your videos exactly what I search for learning new things that are helping me with my disability and not travelling around. So indoor crafts and inventions etc from your videos. They are all very interesting to me more than u would ever realise. A huge thank you for your videos please please keep em
Coming. And your are actually a funny guy. Just the right amount of fun from things you say from time to time you make me laugh and very interested in making things. I’m your die hard FAN 🤞😊👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: hita mate - I live in Canterbury but I also lived in huddersfield for large part of my life - I am glad you like the vids mate all the best, rob

author: @williambianchi2006
text: Started drinking coffee daily when I was 10 years old. (What were my parents thinking?) At this ripe age of 52, I average between one and two pots a day. This is a science experiment to see how much I can take before it kills me. My next of kin will let everyone know when that moment strikes. 😂 Till then, I'll be happily swilling away.
